SOXX — The Lead Scout Has Fallen
What It Means. What Comes Next.

UVCMI 777™ · SOXX Deep Dive · B · ST · IT-LT · 20/20 Vision™ · Fri June 5, 2026
POST #573 · LEAD SCOUT ANALYSIS
⚠️ SOXX −11.14% · UVCMI: −507 · NYSE MO: −27.91
§1 — The Lead Scout Theory

Why SOXX Matters More Than Any Other Sector

SOXX is not just another sector ETF. It is the market's Lead Scout — the first to signal direction for QQQ and SPY. When SOXX leads up, the broad market follows. When SOXX falls — as it did today — it signals what is coming for the indices. This theory, introduced in Post #568 and confirmed in Post #569, is now fully vindicated.

🔭 The Lead Scout Principle

Why semiconductors lead: Semiconductors are the foundational infrastructure of the entire technology economy. Every AI model, every data center, every consumer device runs on chips. TSM, NVDA, AMD, AVGO — the trillion-dollar tech names — all depend on the semiconductor supply chain. When chip demand is accelerating, tech follows. When chips roll over, tech follows.

The sequence: SOXX achieves 7-pillar UVCMI 777™ alignment → QQQ follows within days to weeks → SPY confirms → broad market advances. Conversely: SOXX loses UVCMI alignment → QQQ weakens → SPY confirms → broad market corrects. This sequence played out exactly as described over the past 3 weeks.

Today's confirmation: SOXX −11.14% on the day. B1 W: −1,114 (weekly turning negative). UVCMI D: −507.79. NYSE MO: −27.91. QQQ −5.44%. SPY −2.92%. The Lead Scout fell first — and the broad market followed exactly as the UVCMI 777™ framework predicted.

§2 — SOXX Hourly Chart · Resistance & Support Levels

The Chart That Shows the Structure — R1, R2, S1, S2, S3

SOXX Hourly with UVCMI 777™ and NYSE MO. Dr. UV has marked resistance levels (R1, R2) and support targets (S1, S2, S3) — the roadmap for what comes next.

SOXX Hourly · UVCMI 777™: −507.79 (circled, arrow pointing lower) · NYSE MO: −27.91 (two prior warning circles) · R1: ~$600 · R2: ~$619 · S1 broken · S2: ~$478 · S3: ~$432 · June 5, 2026

⚠️ Chart Critical Signals

S1 broken: SOXX closed at $536 — just above the S1 zone (~$532). A daily close below $532 opens the path to S2 at $478. This is a 10% further decline from current levels — not extreme given today's single-day −11% move, but significant.

UVCMI −507.79 (circled with arrow): The UVCMI cluster is deeply negative and the arrow points lower — no sign of a floor yet. Compare to prior red UVCMI periods on this chart — they resolved after the NYSE MO recovered above zero. MO at −27.91 means we are not there yet.

NYSE MO: −27.91 · Two prior warning circles: Each circled MO below-zero period preceded a SOXX correction. The current reading is the deepest of the three — suggesting this correction has more to run than the prior two.

§3 — B Screen · SOXX vs. All Sectors

SOXX Dead Last — The Highest YTD Gains Being Sold Hardest

Dr. UV's SS36 B Screen · SOXX: −11.14% (bottom) · XSD: −11.91% · XLP: +1.17% (top) · B1 Y: SOXX +7,785 — massive YTD gains being distributed · June 5, 2026

📊 B Screen Read — Distribution of Leaders

The paradox of high B1 Y + massive single-day selloff: SOXX B1 Y: +7,785 — one of the highest YTD readings in the entire SS36 universe. Yet it's the biggest loser today. This is classic distribution: the names that ran the most become the source of funds when risk-off hits. Institutions sell their biggest winners first.

XSD −11.91% · B1 Y: +7,650: The pure semiconductor ETF falls even harder than SOXX. Same dynamic — extreme YTD gains + forced selling = largest single-day loss.

The orange X crossing lines on the B screen: Multiple sectors crossing simultaneously from positive to negative — the visual confirmation that this is not a single-sector event. It is broad-based risk-off rotating FROM tech/semi INTO defensives.

XLP +1.17% leads: Consumer Staples at the top while semiconductors are at the bottom. This is textbook late-cycle defensive rotation — exactly what the NYSE MO divergence warned about for 3+ weeks.

§4 — ST Screen · 1H / 2H / 4H

Short-Term UVCMI — SOXX Most Negative in the Universe

Dr. UV's SOXX Tracker · ST Screen · UVCMI 1H/2H/4H · SOXX 1H: −510 · 2H: −601 · 4H: −127 · Most negative in SS36 universe · Orange X crossing lines · June 5, 2026

📊 ST Screen Read

SOXX 1H: −510 · 2H: −601 · 4H: −127: The most negative short-term readings in the entire SS36 universe. The 2H reading (−601) is especially significant — it means the selling pressure accelerated into the afternoon session, not slowing down. This is not a capitulation spike — it is sustained distribution.

XSD similarly destroyed: 1H: −827 · 2H: −674 · — even worse than SOXX on pure ST readings. The semiconductor index (broader than SOXX) is being hit across every short-term frame without mercy.

Defensive sectors holding ST: XLP, XLU, IDU showing positive or near-zero 1H/2H readings — real money actively rotating into defensive positions intraday. This is not panic selling across all sectors — it is targeted selling of tech/semi with deliberate rotation to safety.

QQQ 1H: −412 · SPY 1H: −148: The spread between QQQ and SPY short-term readings confirms SOXX/tech is driving the selloff. SPY is hurt less because it contains defensive sectors that are holding.

§5 — IT-LT Screen · D / 2D / 3D / W

The Weekly Giant Being Consumed — How Much Cushion Remains

Dr. UV's SOXX Tracker · IT-LT Screen · D/2D/3D/W · SOXX W: +2,091 (massive but being consumed) · SOXX D: +72 (barely positive) · XLP W: +229.8 (defensive weekly intact) · June 5, 2026

📊 IT-LT Screen Read — The Weekly Cushion

SOXX W: +2,091 — still positive but watch it closely: SOXX has accumulated a massive positive weekly UVCMI reading from months of leadership. Today's D reading (+72 — barely positive) is being consumed by the daily negative pressure. If the daily remains negative for 3-5 more sessions, the weekly will flip negative. That weekly flip is the key signal to watch — it would mark SOXX moving from "correcting within a bull trend" to "intermediate-term bearish."

SOXX D: +72 vs. D: −507 on hourly: The daily chart UVCMI is still marginally positive (D: +72) while the hourly (UVCMI panel on the chart: −507) is deeply negative. This gap will close — either the daily gets dragged down by the hourly pressure, or the hourly recovers. Currently the momentum strongly favors the daily turning negative.

XLP W: +229.8 · XLU W: +327 · XLRE W: +415: Defensive sector weekly UVCMI readings are holding positive and showing relative strength. This confirms the defensive rotation has intermediate-term legs — not just a one-session flight to safety.

QQQ W: still positive (+996): The broad tech benchmark weekly is positive but softening. SPY W: +681. These positive weekly readings mean the primary bull trend is intact — but under increasing pressure. The correction is intermediate-term, not a bear market signal — unless weekly readings flip negative across the board.

§6 — UVCMI 20/20 Vision™

20 Timeframes — The Crossing Curves Tell the Full Story

Dr. UV's SOXX Tracker · UVCMI 20/20 Vision™ · 20 Simultaneous Timeframes · SOXX highlighted blue (bottom rows) · Massive orange curve formations · Cross-sector timeframe reversal visible · June 5, 2026

👁️ 20/20 Vision™ Read — The Curves

The orange curves (not just X lines): In this 20/20 Vision™ view, the orange lines form sweeping curves — not just crossing X patterns. These curves represent sustained momentum vectors transitioning over multiple timeframes simultaneously. A sharp X suggests a single-session reversal. A sweeping curve suggests a multi-session, multi-timeframe momentum transition — which is more significant and more durable.

SOXX (blue highlighted rows): SOXX sits at the bottom of this screen — and its curve is the most dramatic, sweeping from positive to negative across the longest timeframe range visible. The width of the sweep indicates how many timeframes are simultaneously transitioning — for SOXX, it is nearly all of them.

The separation above and below the SOXX row: Above SOXX, defensive sectors show curves transitioning the other direction — positive momentum building. Below SOXX, other tech/semi names show similar negative sweeps. The visual separation between defensive (top) and tech/semi (bottom) has never been clearer in this tracker.

§7 — Full UVCMI 777™ · All Components

Sea of Red — Systemic Selling Across SOXX Components

Dr. UV's SOXX Tracker · UVCMI 777™ All Timeframes · All SOXX component stocks · Overwhelmingly red across every timeframe · Systemic distribution confirmed · SOXX highlighted (top rows) · June 5, 2026

📊 All UVCMI 777™ Read — No Safe Names in SOXX

The sea of red: This screen shows individual SOXX component stocks with their UVCMI readings across all 7 timeframes. The overwhelming red across virtually every component — not just the ETF — confirms this is fundamental selling of the semiconductor sector, not just ETF-level rebalancing.

Why this matters: When only the ETF is red but components are mixed, it can be index-level technical selling. When the individual components are all red simultaneously across multiple timeframes, it is institutional distribution of the underlying stocks. That is what this screen confirms — the selling is real and broad-based within the semiconductor complex.

No green islands visible: In a normal correction, some component stocks hold up — creating visible green clusters within the red. Here, there are almost none. This level of uniform red across all SOXX components in all timeframes is the definition of sector-wide distribution.

§8 — Re-Entry Criteria

When Will SOXX Signal a Return? — The UVCMI 777™ Checklist

SOXX does not give you timing — it gives you signals. Here are the specific UVCMI 777™ conditions required before considering re-entry into semiconductor names.

🎯 SOXX Re-Entry Checklist — UVCMI 777™

1
NYSE MO recovers above zero. Currently −27.91. The McClellan Oscillator must cross back above the zero line and hold there for at least 3 sessions. This is the primary breadth confirmation.
2
SOXX UVCMI D recovers from negative. The daily UVCMI (currently +72 but pressured) must rebuild a green cluster — not just a single green bar, but a sustained cluster of 5+ green bars showing consistent buying pressure.
3
SOXX UVCMI W holds positive. The weekly reading (+2,091 currently) must not flip negative. If W goes negative, the re-entry criteria reset to a longer timeframe — this would become an intermediate-term bearish signal, not just a correction.
4
SOXX price reclaims S1 (~$532) AND holds it for 2 sessions. A close back above $532 with UVCMI recovering is the price confirmation to accompany the momentum signal.
5
ST Screen 1H/2H turn green. The short-term frames (currently 1H: −510, 2H: −601) must recover to positive. This is the earliest signal — it will appear before the daily chart shows recovery. Watch for 1H and 2H both green simultaneously.
6
QQQ ST frames recover alongside SOXX. Per the Lead Scout principle, SOXX leads QQQ. But confirming QQQ's 1H/2H also turning green adds confidence that the recovery is broad-based, not just a SOXX dead-cat bounce.

📅 Weekend Watchlist — What to Monitor

Monday pre-market: NYSE futures UVCMI in 1H/2H — are sellers continuing or taking a weekend pause? The first hour Monday sets the tone.

Support to watch: SOXX $532 (S1) — if this holds Monday, potential stabilization. If $532 breaks on volume, next target is $478 (S2).

Key names within SOXX: TSM, NVDA, AMD, AVGO — watch their individual UVCMI 1H readings. When 3 of 4 turn green simultaneously, SOXX will follow.

Safe harbor remains active: XLP, XLU, XLRE, XLV — defensive sectors with intact weekly UVCMI. These are the UVCMI 777™ Safe Harbor per the 2-Step Exit rule from Post #568. Hold until SOXX re-entry criteria are met.
